description: This figure contains two transmission electron microscopy (TEM) micrographs of deformed Fe-25at.% Al alloy with DO3 and B2 long-range order. The left image is a dark-field (DF) micrograph acquired using the 100 diffraction beam under Bragg condition, while the right image is a weak-beam dark-field (WBDF) micrograph with a 0.2 μm scale bar, acquired using the 200 beam with 400 beam excited to enable both fine dislocation contrast and residual anti-phase boundary (APB) contrast, with the arrow indicating the <100> imaging g vector. In the left DF image, 't' labels thermal APBs from coarse B2 domains, and 's' labels deformation-induced APBI faults formed by glide of 1/2<111> dislocations. Both micrographs show loosely coupled dislocation pairs connected by low-energy APBs, verifying that APB energies in this alloy are insufficiently high to form closely coupled superdislocations, which supports the interpretation of work hardening behavior in ordered Fe-Al alloys.
